_comment: The list of connections your always-on agent depends on. Each check is a plain shell command that MUST exit 0 when healthy and non-zero when down. Exit 20 means 'blocked, needs you'. NEVER paste a token here — read auth from your environment or your CLI's own stored login. Delete the checks you don't use, edit the commands to match your setup, and add your own.

checks.name: model CLI reachable
checks._comment: Proves the agent can still talk to its model. Swap in a cheap health call your CLI supports; the point is that a spent budget or a dead endpoint makes this exit non-zero.
checks.command: claude -p --output-format text --max-budget-usd 0.01 'reply with the single word OK' | grep -qi ok

checks.name: MCP login (example CRM)
checks._comment: Proves an MCP/tool login still authenticates RIGHT NOW. Replace with a tiny read-only 'whoami'/list call for your own tool that fails when the session has expired. An expired login exits non-zero here and pings you.

checks.name: scheduled run making progress
checks._comment: Proves the overnight run is not just alive but actually MOVING. Point watch_path at the folder the run writes into (e.g. its reports dir). If nothing there changes for stall_minutes, it flips to STALLED. Leave 'command' empty to judge purely by output movement, or set it to a check that exits 20 when the run is blocked on you.

checks.name: agent process alive (optional)
checks._comment: Optional: proves the scheduled process is still running. Replace 'my-agent-run' with a unique string from your own job's command line. A dead process exits non-zero.
